description: Wave 9b - Human Multiplayer v1 DISCLOSURE + LOBBIES + FRONTEND + E2E (US-141..157), second sub-wave. ASSUMES v9a (US-121..140) is merged to main: cross-sub-wave dependencies on v9a stories were removed (they are satisfied externally); only intra-v9b deps remain. Build order: P4 disclosure/endgame/stats + CI invariant gates (141-146) -> P5 lobbies/takeover/cost/sprites/frontend + e2e (147-157). Same two invariants as v9a. Human-gated prep (OAuth app, OFFLINE static sprite generation, legal copy, cap numbers) is NOT stories - see ralph/PREP-v9-human-multiplayer.md.

userStories.title: Identity-mode-aware observation + projection
userStories.description: As the platform, a playing seat's view must reveal model/human identity only in transparent mode and never in anonymous mode, defaulting closed.
acceptanceCriteria: An identity-mode-aware build_observation variant: in ANONYMOUS a playing seat's view has ZERO model/provider/agent-build identifiers AND zero human-vs-AI markers; in TRANSPARENT it may surface them while still hiding other seats' roles/factions; the spectator/public projection is likewise mode-aware.
A missing/None mode coerces to ANONYMOUS (fail closed).
tests/core/test_mode_aware_observation.py: anonymous leaks nothing, transparent surfaces allowed fields, None coerces to anonymous.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Composition-counts disclosure (counts only, frozen)
userStories.description: As a player/spectator, I see only how many humans vs AI are present, never which seat is which, and the counts do not change on a silent takeover.
acceptanceCriteria: Every player/spectator/lobby surface that shows composition uses the single canonical composition_summary (US-126): counts only, frozen at game start, never a per-seat map; a silent AI takeover does NOT change displayed counts.
tests/api/test_composition_counts.py asserts counts-only across lobby + live + spectator surfaces and frozen counts across a takeover.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Endgame reveal projection + terminal-gated endpoint
userStories.description: As a player, at the end of a game I always see the full truth: which seats were human, each seat's role/faction, the exact models, and any takeover - and never one event early.
acceptanceCriteria: A terminal-only reveal projection assembles per-seat {is_human, role, faction, exact model for AI seats, takeover provenance HUMAN/AI/HUMAN_THEN_AI + phase} from seat data + event log; ONE canonical reveal schema (the review found 5 divergent definitions).
GET /public/games/{id}/reveal returns it ONLY when the game is terminal/RECENT; 404/empty while LIVE; always reveals even in anonymous mode (decision 11).
tests/api/test_endgame_reveal.py: LIVE returns no reveal; terminal returns full per-seat truth including takeover provenance.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Spot-the-AI guess + pure scorer + personal stat
userStories.description: As a human player, after the game I guess which seats were AI and see my detection accuracy - the core imitation-game hook - without any competitive leaderboard in v1.
acceptanceCriteria: After terminal, each human submits one guess assigning HUMAN/AI to every other seat over the existing human channel (a thin post-terminal step, NOT a heavyweight new FSM phase); core/turing/scoring.py (pure, no clock/random/IO) computes per-guesser detection accuracy; guess + result persist.
The reveal endpoint gates the viewer's own accuracy result behind their submitted guess; NO cross-player/cross-model leaderboards in v1.
tests/core/test_turing_scoring.py + tests/api/test_guess_flow.py cover scoring and the guess-then-reveal gating.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P4. Keep the hook + personal stat (decision 6); competitive Turing leaderboards deferred per the review.

userStories.title: Per-human stats from the event log
userStories.description: As a signed-in player, my play history yields stats (win rate by role/faction, survival, voting accuracy, detection accuracy) computed deterministically - with no leaderboard or ELO in v1.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend padrino.analytics.deterministic (pure) with a participant-keyed extractor taking an injected {public_player_id -> principal_id} map; persist a human_player_stats aggregate keyed by (ruleset_id, principal_id): games, wins/draws/losses, role/faction win rates, survival rate, voting accuracy, detection accuracy.
Never written for scientific leagues; no leaderboard/ELO surface in v1.
tests/analytics/test_human_stats.py covers deterministic computation and that scientific leagues are untouched.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P4. Stats captured (accounts persist them); leaderboards/ELO deferred to a fast-follow.

userStories.title: Anonymity + segregation CI gates
userStories.description: As the platform, the two non-negotiable invariants must be suite-blocking so no future change can regress anonymity or pollute the benchmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Promote the anonymity property test (US-124) and segregation test (US-125) to comprehensive gates covering every live frame, observation, pre-terminal reveal, and export path: a completed human game provably leaks zero human-vs-AI/model markers pre-reveal and writes zero scientific rating rows.
tests/test_invariants_human_mp.py aggregates the property + segregation checks and runs in the default suite.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P4. The two invariants enforced in CI from here on (the review's #3 top risk).

userStories.title: Lobby tables + create/configure
userStories.description: As a host, I create a private lobby and configure its game (ruleset/size, identity mode, theme, bot pre-pick vs auto-fill, casual stakes).
acceptanceCriteria: New tables lobbies, lobby_members, lobby_seats (per the schema in prd-v3.md): lobby holds ruleset_id, identity_mode, theme_pack_id, stakes pinned CASUAL, status OPEN|LOCKED|LAUNCHED|CLOSED, lobby_seed, host, league_id (Humans-Included), nullable game_id.

POST /lobbies (create + host config: ruleset/size in {mini7_v1, bench10_v1}, identity_mode, theme_pack_id from the static library, pre-pick human_eligible models OR curated auto-fill, stakes=CASUAL); GET /lobbies/{id} member-scoped with count-only composition.
Migration chains after the latest v9 schema head; tests/api/test_lobby_create.py.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5. Private friend lobbies only (decision 2). No public matchmaking.

userStories.title: Invite links, roster, ready-up, presence
userStories.description: As a host, I share an invite link; friends (guest or signed-in) join, ready up, and I can lock and launch; idle lobbies auto-cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /lobbies/join/{invite_token} (guest- and account-joinable, single-use-per-person via membership), roster read, ready-up, host lock/kick/transfer; heartbeat presence with stale-member eviction; idle/host-abandon auto-cancel lifecycle.
A member-scoped lobby state channel streams roster/ready/presence deltas, identity-blind (never per-seat human/AI in anonymous mode).
tests/api/test_lobby_invite_presence.py covers join, ready, presence eviction, and auto-cancel.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Deterministic curated auto-fill + launch handoff
userStories.description: As the platform, empty seats fill deterministically from a curated cheap-model pool and the lobby launches a real game on the human worker lane.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure src/padrino/core/lobby/autofill.py deterministically assigns curated human_eligible AgentBuild ids to EMPTY seats given (lobby_seed, reserved map, curated roster), SeededRng only (no random/clock/IO), raising when the pool is smaller than the empty seats.

Launch handoff materializes a Game + GameSeat rows (humans -> occupant_principal_id with seat_kind HUMAN, AI -> agent_build_id with seat_kind AI) on the human worker lane (US-132); launch is single-fire/idempotent.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Disconnect grace + silent AI takeover
userStories.description: As a player, if I drop I get a short grace window to reconnect; otherwise an AI silently takes my seat so the game continues, invisibly in anonymous mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-seat presence over the human transport; a reconnect grace window with identity-blind full-state replay; on grace expiry a curated AI silently takes over (SeatMultiplexAdapter swap; emit SeatTakenOver; reveal-only). Reconnect before takeover returns the seat; after takeover returns an identity-blind spectator.
In anonymous mode, other-seat presence/reconnecting state is never exposed (AIs do not disconnect); takeover is invisible mid-game and appears only in the endgame reveal provenance.
tests/runner/test_disconnect_takeover.py: the game progresses through a takeover, no mid-game leak, provenance shown at reveal.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decision 9 (v1 casual; ranked leaver penalties dormant).

userStories.title: Cost governance: caps + throttle-not-kill breaker
userStories.description: As the operator, human play is platform-absorbed within a Moderate budget; caps and a circuit breaker bound spend by stopping NEW lobbies, never by killing an active game.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-user/day game + join caps and a per-user/day inference-$ cap keyed on principal (OAuth account else hashed guest token), enforced at lobby create/join/launch admission; curated human_eligible model pool; fallback token-price table when LiteLLM response_cost is None.

A per-lobby cost cap + circuit breaker that, on breach, STOPS new lobbies / new LLM turns but LETS ACTIVE GAMES FINISH (never boots humans); add a funding_source column (PLATFORM|BYOK_OWNER|SPONSOR_POOL, default PLATFORM) to the cost-tracking row (BYOK/sponsor otherwise dormant).
tests/economics/test_human_cost_governance.py: admission denies past caps, the breaker throttles new lobbies but never kills an active game, funding_source is recorded.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decisions 14/19/20. The reviewers' 'AI-only continuation boots humans' anti-pattern is explicitly rejected. Concrete cap numbers are a PREP-v9 sign-off.

userStories.title: Static themed sprite library
userStories.description: As a player, seats show themed sprites from a curated static library - no runtime image generation - and in anonymous mode the sprite can never encode role or human/AI.
acceptanceCriteria: A curated set of pre-generated themed sprite assets (3-5 theme packs) shipped as static files + a manifest, served read-only with immutable Cache-Control via a new asset route; anonymous mode maps every seat to a role-agnostic archetype per theme; deterministic placeholder fallback.
NO runtime image generation in v1 (the gemini-3.1-flash-image authoring step is offline/human - see PREP-v9).
tests/api/test_sprite_library.py: the manifest serves, anonymous archetypes are role-agnostic, an unknown key 404s.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decision 13 delivered as a static library; the review cut the runtime pipeline.

userStories.title: Frontend live transport client
userStories.description: As the dashboard, I gain a bidirectional play client (SSE-out + authenticated POST-in) with reconnect-by-sequence and a human session credential, turning the observe-only app interactive.
acceptanceCriteria: web/dashboard: a liveClient (SSE-out + authenticated POST-in) with reconnect-by-sequence + idempotency keys, and a play-session store (Svelte 5 runes) deriving board/votes/chat from the released frame stream; PadrinoClient gains a human session/guest credential mode alongside the existing spectator API key.

web/dashboard/src/lib/api gains types + client methods for the live seat stream, action POST, chat POST, lobby, reveal, guess, and human stats; vitest unit coverage parallels the existing client tests.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5 / frontend. SSE+POST (decision: not raw WebSocket) - fits the adapter-static SPA.

userStories.title: Frontend lobby UI
userStories.description: As a host/guest, I create or join a lobby from an invite link, set it up, accept the one-tap consent, and ready up.
acceptanceCriteria: /lobby routes: create form (ruleset/size, identity mode, theme pack, bot pre-pick vs auto-fill, stakes shown CASUAL), invite-link share, guest-quickplay join with no signup + optional sign-in CTA + the one-tap consent + 16+ gate, live roster with count-only composition, ready/host controls, start.
Playwright web/dashboard/tests/e2e/lobby.spec.ts covers guest-join -> consent -> ready against the smoke harness.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Frontend in-game play surface
userStories.description: As a player, I see the board, my legal actions, the phase timer, and a buffered chat that shows messages only after release - all identity-blind in anonymous mode.
acceptanceCriteria: /play/[gameId]: seat board (identity-blind, count-only composition, themed static sprites), phase + countdown header (non-precise so it does not leak timing), legal-action-gated action panel + vote panel + role-conditional night panel, a buffered chat composer with pending/blocked/released states and a feed fed ONLY by released frames; reconnect with full-state replay.
Playwright web/dashboard/tests/e2e/play.spec.ts covers a scripted game turn (vote + a released chat message) and asserts no identity leak in anonymous mode.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: Frontend endgame reveal + guess + profile
userStories.description: As a player, at game end I see the full reveal, submit my spot-the-AI guess, see my detection accuracy, and (if signed in) view my stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Endgame reveal screen (per-seat human/AI, role, faction, exact model, takeover provenance, themed sprite); the spot-the-AI guess UI (per-seat HUMAN/AI toggles, single submit, gating the reveal of own accuracy); a minimal profile/stats page for signed-in accounts (casual framing, no live ELO).
Playwright web/dashboard/tests/e2e/reveal.spec.ts covers guess-then-reveal and the stats page.
uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: End-to-end human-game smoke test
userStories.description: As the platform, one default-suite test must exercise the ENTIRE human spine - lobby through reveal - so a cross-boundary contract break can never pass a green suite (the Wave-7 lesson).
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_human_game_e2e.py (default suite; mock LLM adapter + a scripted human client driving the POST channels; zero/short delays): create lobby -> guest join -> consent -> ready -> auto-fill -> play a full game through votes + night actions + one moderated chat message -> terminal -> endgame reveal exposes models + human seats -> a spot-the-AI guess scores.
The test asserts anonymity held on every live frame (no forbidden keys) and that ZERO scientific Rating/RatingEvent rows were written.
uv run mypy src tests passes
userStories.notes: P5 verification. The single end-to-end guard for the whole human lane; closes the wave.
